script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Welcome to DataBard! Today we're diving into the ecommerce schema — 4 tables to explore. Let's get into it.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: I've been looking at the quality scores and lineage for this one. There's some interesting stuff — and a few things we need to talk about.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Next up is orders. The core transaction table — every purchase flows through here. It has 6 columns including order_id, customer_id, total_amount and more.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Red flag on orders. 1 of 3 quality tests are failing: not_null_order_id. 2 passing. This needs attention.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Next up is customers. Our customer master data — names, emails, signup dates. It has customer_id as bigint, email as varchar, created_at as timestamp.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It's tagged with PII, retention-policy — so the team has been classifying this one.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Good news — all 2 quality tests on customers are passing. Solid.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Next up is products. The product catalog — SKUs, prices, categories. It has 5 columns including product_id, name, price and more.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: No quality tests on products. That's a gap. We're flying blind on data integrity here.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Next up is order_items. Line items linking orders to products. It has item_id, order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Red flag on order_items. 1 of 2 quality tests are failing: positive_quantity. 1 passing. This needs attention.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Let's talk data flow. We've got 3 lineage connections. customers feeds into orders. products feeds into order_items. orders feeds into order_items.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: That lineage map tells you where a problem in one table cascades. Worth reviewing if any of those upstream tables have failing tests.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's the ecommerce schema — 4 tables, 7 quality tests, 3 lineage edges. Thanks for listening.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: And 2 failing tests to go fix. Don't ignore those. Until next time.
